description: CRIT-801's threshold (weight 0.08) requires `grep -n "EC-IPC-" promptnotes/src-tauri/tests/` to return matches covering at least EC-IPC-001 through EC-IPC-014 (14 distinct IDs). Inspection of `tests/editor_wire_sprint8.rs` and the other Rust tests under `promptnotes/src-tauri/tests/` finds no `EC-IPC-NNN` substrings. Test names follow the `prop_ipc_NNN_*` convention and the docstrings document only PROP-IPC obligations; edge cases EC-IPC-001..014 are folded into the §10.2.1 14-fixture cover set (one fixture per row) but the EC-IPC ID itself is not used as an inline tag. As with FIND-088, the threshold's grep cannot find any of the 14 required IDs and CRIT-801 is mechanically unsatisfiable at Phase 5. The contract should either (a) drop the EC-IPC grep requirement and instead require the cover-set table to be enumerated (e.g. `jq 'length' wire-fixtures.json == 14` plus per-fixture key-set assertions, which CRIT-803 already covers), or (b) require Phase 2b to add inline `// EC-IPC-NNN` tags to the Rust fixtures and assertions before grading.

evidence.snippet:
passThreshold: >
  `grep -n "EC-IPC-" promptnotes/src-tauri/tests/` returns matches covering at least
  EC-IPC-001 through EC-IPC-014 (14 distinct IDs). Each test that references an EC-IPC
  ID has at least one assertion against the documented behaviour.
